forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,36 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: '9080'
+project: ai-panel
+title: Enable ACP elicitation types in the workspace (schema unstable_elicitation + acp 0.12 bump)
+---
+#elicitation
+
+## Context / Why
+Prerequisite for both Rust agent-wrapper elicitation bridges. The ACP elicitation types (`CreateElicitationRequest`, `CreateElicitationResponse`, `ElicitationAction`) live in the `agent-client-protocol-schema` crate behind its `unstable_elicitation` feature. The user approved enabling the unstable feature; this is the deferred follow-up the board's "ACP 0.10 → 0.11 upgrade" project explicitly left open ("Excludes opting into the `unstable` feature unless a follow-up task chooses to").
+
+## Verified findings (so the implementer doesn't re-derive)
+- The **main** `agent-client-protocol` crate does NOT expose/forward an `unstable_elicitation` feature at any available version (checked 0.11.1, 0.12.0, 0.12.1) — its `[features]` forwards 10 other `unstable_*` features but not elicitation. So it must be enabled on the **schema** crate directly.
+- `agent-client-protocol-schema` 0.12.0 defines `unstable_elicitation = []` and gates `CreateElicitationRequest`/`CreateElicitationResponse`/`ElicitationAction` plus `ELICITATION_CREATE_METHOD_NAME = "elicitation/create"` and `ELICITATION_COMPLETE_NOTIFICATION = "elicitation/complete"` behind it (see `src/client.rs`).
+- These method names MATCH the TS SDK (`elicitation/create`, `elicitation/complete`) — no cross-SDK reconciliation needed.
+- `Cargo.lock` already resolves `agent-client-protocol-schema = 0.12.0` (main crate still 0.11.1).
+- Workspace `Cargo.toml` opens with "NEVER add features or feature flags to this workspace!" — that rule targets gating SAH functionality; enabling a third-party dependency feature **always-on** (no optionality) is consistent with "all functionality all the time". Keep it unconditional.
+
+## What
+- [ ] Bump `agent-client-protocol` to `0.12.1` in the workspace `Cargo.toml` (aligns the main crate with schema 0.12; this is the deferred ACP-upgrade follow-up).
+- [ ] Add `agent-client-protocol-schema = { version = "0.12", features = ["unstable_elicitation"] }` as a direct workspace dependency so Cargo feature-unification turns the elicitation types on through the main crate's `schema` re-export. Confirm `agent_client_protocol::schema::CreateElicitationRequest` resolves (the main crate re-exports the schema crate).
+- [ ] Rebuild the 7 ACP-using crates and fix any 0.11→0.12 API drift: `agent-client-protocol-extras`, `claude-agent`, `llama-agent`, `acp-conformance`, `swissarmyhammer-agent`, `swissarmyhammer-tools`, `avp-common`.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] `agent_client_protocol::schema::{CreateElicitationRequest, CreateElicitationResponse, ElicitationAction}` compile and are usable from `claude-agent` and `llama-agent`.
+- [ ] `cargo build --workspace` is green; no other crate regressed by the version bump.
+- [ ] The elicitation feature is unconditional (not behind a SAH-defined feature flag).
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] A compile-level test (e.g. in `agent-client-protocol-extras` or `swissarmyhammer-agent`) that constructs a `CreateElicitationRequest` and matches on `ElicitationAction`, proving the types are reachable.
+- [ ] Run: `cargo nextest run -p agent-client-protocol-extras -p claude-agent -p llama-agent -p swissarmyhammer-agent` — all green.
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` where it fits (the compile test first). Treat 0.11→0.12 API drift as part of this task.
\ No newline at end of file

-assignees:
-- claude-code
-position_column: todo
-position_ordinal: '9080'
-project: ai-panel
-title: Enable ACP elicitation types in the workspace (schema unstable_elicitation + acp 0.12 bump)
----
-#elicitation
-
-## Context / Why
-Prerequisite for both Rust agent-wrapper elicitation bridges. The ACP elicitation types (`CreateElicitationRequest`, `CreateElicitationResponse`, `ElicitationAction`) live in the `agent-client-protocol-schema` crate behind its `unstable_elicitation` feature. The user approved enabling the unstable feature; this is the deferred follow-up the board's "ACP 0.10 → 0.11 upgrade" project explicitly left open ("Excludes opting into the `unstable` feature unless a follow-up task chooses to").
-
-## Verified findings (so the implementer doesn't re-derive)
-- The **main** `agent-client-protocol` crate does NOT expose/forward an `unstable_elicitation` feature at any available version (checked 0.11.1, 0.12.0, 0.12.1) — its `[features]` forwards 10 other `unstable_*` features but not elicitation. So it must be enabled on the **schema** crate directly.
-- `agent-client-protocol-schema` 0.12.0 defines `unstable_elicitation = []` and gates `CreateElicitationRequest`/`CreateElicitationResponse`/`ElicitationAction` plus `ELICITATION_CREATE_METHOD_NAME = "elicitation/create"` and `ELICITATION_COMPLETE_NOTIFICATION = "elicitation/complete"` behind it (see `src/client.rs`).
-- These method names MATCH the TS SDK (`elicitation/create`, `elicitation/complete`) — no cross-SDK reconciliation needed.
-- `Cargo.lock` already resolves `agent-client-protocol-schema = 0.12.0` (main crate still 0.11.1).
-- Workspace `Cargo.toml` opens with "NEVER add features or feature flags to this workspace!" — that rule targets gating SAH functionality; enabling a third-party dependency feature **always-on** (no optionality) is consistent with "all functionality all the time". Keep it unconditional.
-
-## What
-- [ ] Bump `agent-client-protocol` to `0.12.1` in the workspace `Cargo.toml` (aligns the main crate with schema 0.12; this is the deferred ACP-upgrade follow-up).
-- [ ] Add `agent-client-protocol-schema = { version = "0.12", features = ["unstable_elicitation"] }` as a direct workspace dependency so Cargo feature-unification turns the elicitation types on through the main crate's `schema` re-export. Confirm `agent_client_protocol::schema::CreateElicitationRequest` resolves (the main crate re-exports the schema crate).
-- [ ] Rebuild the 7 ACP-using crates and fix any 0.11→0.12 API drift: `agent-client-protocol-extras`, `claude-agent`, `llama-agent`, `acp-conformance`, `swissarmyhammer-agent`, `swissarmyhammer-tools`, `avp-common`.
-
-## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] `agent_client_protocol::schema::{CreateElicitationRequest, CreateElicitationResponse, ElicitationAction}` compile and are usable from `claude-agent` and `llama-agent`.
-- [ ] `cargo build --workspace` is green; no other crate regressed by the version bump.
-- [ ] The elicitation feature is unconditional (not behind a SAH-defined feature flag).
-
-## Tests
-- [ ] A compile-level test (e.g. in `agent-client-protocol-extras` or `swissarmyhammer-agent`) that constructs a `CreateElicitationRequest` and matches on `ElicitationAction`, proving the types are reachable.
-- [ ] Run: `cargo nextest run -p agent-client-protocol-extras -p claude-agent -p llama-agent -p swissarmyhammer-agent` — all green.
-
-## Workflow
-- Use `/tdd` where it fits (the compile test first). Treat 0.11→0.12 API drift as part of this task.
\ No newline at end of file

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -18,19 +18,26 @@
 - `Cargo.lock` already resolves `agent-client-protocol-schema = 0.12.0` (main crate still 0.11.1).
 - Workspace `Cargo.toml` opens with "NEVER add features or feature flags to this workspace!" — that rule targets gating SAH functionality; enabling a third-party dependency feature **always-on** (no optionality) is consistent with "all functionality all the time". Keep it unconditional.

+## IMPLEMENTATION NOTE — version bump was UNNECESSARY (smaller change taken)
+The literal "What" said to bump `agent-client-protocol` to 0.12.1. While implementing I verified that **main 0.12.1 pins `agent-client-protocol-schema = "=0.13.2"`** (an exact-version pin), whereas **main 0.11.1 pins `=0.12.0`**. So bumping the main crate would have forced schema 0.13.2 and introduced 0.11→0.12 API-drift risk across 7 crates — for no benefit, since the dependents only need the elicitation *types reachable*, not any new 0.12 main-crate API.
+
+Per the task's own escape hatch ("If the version bump turns out to be unnecessary … prefer the smaller change and document what you did"), I took **Path A**: keep `agent-client-protocol = "0.11"` and add `agent-client-protocol-schema = { version = "=0.12.0", features = ["unstable_elicitation"] }` to `[workspace.dependencies]`, then reference it from `agent-client-protocol-extras` so Cargo feature-unification turns the types on through the main crate's `agent_client_protocol::schema` re-export (`pub use agent_client_protocol_schema::*`).
+
+Result: minimal change (Cargo.lock gains exactly one line — schema added to extras' dep list; schema stays 0.12.0; no main-crate bump; zero API drift). Every ACP-using crate (`claude-agent`, `llama-agent`, `avp-common`, `acp-conformance`, `swissarmyhammer-agent`) depends on `agent-client-protocol-extras`, so the feature unifies for all of them. `cargo tree -e features` confirms `agent-client-protocol-schema v0.12.0` builds with `feature "unstable_elicitation"`.
+
 ## What
-- [ ] Bump `agent-client-protocol` to `0.12.1` in the workspace `Cargo.toml` (aligns the main crate with schema 0.12; this is the deferred ACP-upgrade follow-up).
-- [ ] Add `agent-client-protocol-schema = { version = "0.12", features = ["unstable_elicitation"] }` as a direct workspace dependency so Cargo feature-unification turns the elicitation types on through the main crate's `schema` re-export. Confirm `agent_client_protocol::schema::CreateElicitationRequest` resolves (the main crate re-exports the schema crate).
-- [ ] Rebuild the 7 ACP-using crates and fix any 0.11→0.12 API drift: `agent-client-protocol-extras`, `claude-agent`, `llama-agent`, `acp-conformance`, `swissarmyhammer-agent`, `swissarmyhammer-tools`, `avp-common`.
+- [x] ~~Bump `agent-client-protocol` to `0.12.1`~~ — NOT done: verified unnecessary and counterproductive (0.12.1 pins schema `=0.13.2`, forcing API drift). Smaller Path A taken instead — see Implementation Note.
+- [x] Add `agent-client-protocol-schema = { version = "=0.12.0", features = ["unstable_elicitation"] }` as a direct workspace dependency (and reference it from `agent-client-protocol-extras`) so Cargo feature-unification turns the elicitation types on through the main crate's `schema` re-export. Confirmed `agent_client_protocol::schema::CreateElicitationRequest` resolves.
+- [x] Rebuild the ACP-using crates — no 0.11→0.12 API drift to fix (main crate stayed at 0.11.1). `cargo build --workspace` green.

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] `agent_client_protocol::schema::{CreateElicitationRequest, CreateElicitationResponse, ElicitationAction}` compile and are usable from `claude-agent` and `llama-agent`.
-- [ ] `cargo build --workspace` is green; no other crate regressed by the version bump.
-- [ ] The elicitation feature is unconditional (not behind a SAH-defined feature flag).
+- [x] `agent_client_protocol::schema::{CreateElicitationRequest, CreateElicitationResponse, ElicitationAction}` compile and are usable from `claude-agent` and `llama-agent` (verified via isolated `cargo build -p claude-agent -p llama-agent` + `cargo tree -e features`).
+- [x] `cargo build --workspace` is green; no other crate regressed.
+- [x] The elicitation feature is unconditional (not behind a SAH-defined feature flag).

 ## Tests
-- [ ] A compile-level test (e.g. in `agent-client-protocol-extras` or `swissarmyhammer-agent`) that constructs a `CreateElicitationRequest` and matches on `ElicitationAction`, proving the types are reachable.
-- [ ] Run: `cargo nextest run -p agent-client-protocol-extras -p claude-agent -p llama-agent -p swissarmyhammer-agent` — all green.
+- [x] Compile-level test in `agent-client-protocol-extras` (`tests/elicitation_types_reachable.rs`) that constructs `CreateElicitationResponse` and matches on `ElicitationAction`, naming `CreateElicitationRequest`, proving the types are reachable. Written TDD: confirmed RED (E0432 unresolved imports) before the feature wiring, GREEN after.
+- [x] Run: `cargo nextest run -p agent-client-protocol-extras -p claude-agent -p llama-agent -p swissarmyhammer-agent` — 2428 tests, all green.

 ## Workflow
 - Use `/tdd` where it fits (the compile test first). Treat 0.11→0.12 API drift as part of this task.
\ No newline at end of file

-- [x] Add `agent-client-protocol-schema = { version = "=0.12.0", features = ["unstable_elicitation"] }` as a direct workspace dependency (and reference it from `agent-client-protocol-extras`) so Cargo feature-unification turns the elicitation types on through the main crate's `schema` re-export. Confirmed `agent_client_protocol::schema::CreateElicitationRequest` resolves.
-- [x] Rebuild the ACP-using crates — no 0.11→0.12 API drift to fix (main crate stayed at 0.11.1). `cargo build --workspace` green.
+- [ ] Bump `agent-client-protocol` to `0.12.1` in the workspace `Cargo.toml` (aligns the main crate with schema 0.12; this is the deferred ACP-upgrade follow-up).
+- [ ] Add `agent-client-protocol-schema = { version = "0.12", features = ["unstable_elicitation"] }` as a direct workspace dependency so Cargo feature-unification turns the elicitation types on through the main crate's `schema` re-export. Confirm `agent_client_protocol::schema::CreateElicitationRequest` resolves (the main crate re-exports the schema crate).
+- [ ] Rebuild the 7 ACP-using crates and fix any 0.11→0.12 API drift: `agent-client-protocol-extras`, `claude-agent`, `llama-agent`, `acp-conformance`, `swissarmyhammer-agent`, `swissarmyhammer-tools`, `avp-common`.

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [x] `agent_client_protocol::schema::{CreateElicitationRequest, CreateElicitationResponse, ElicitationAction}` compile and are usable from `claude-agent` and `llama-agent` (verified via isolated `cargo build -p claude-agent -p llama-agent` + `cargo tree -e features`).
-- [x] `cargo build --workspace` is green; no other crate regressed.
-- [x] The elicitation feature is unconditional (not behind a SAH-defined feature flag).
+- [ ] `agent_client_protocol::schema::{CreateElicitationRequest, CreateElicitationResponse, ElicitationAction}` compile and are usable from `claude-agent` and `llama-agent`.
+- [ ] `cargo build --workspace` is green; no other crate regressed by the version bump.
+- [ ] The elicitation feature is unconditional (not behind a SAH-defined feature flag).

 ## Tests
-- [x] Compile-level test in `agent-client-protocol-extras` (`tests/elicitation_types_reachable.rs`) that constructs `CreateElicitationResponse` and matches on `ElicitationAction`, naming `CreateElicitationRequest`, proving the types are reachable. Written TDD: confirmed RED (E0432 unresolved imports) before the feature wiring, GREEN after.
-- [x] Run: `cargo nextest run -p agent-client-protocol-extras -p claude-agent -p llama-agent -p swissarmyhammer-agent` — 2428 tests, all green.
+- [ ] A compile-level test (e.g. in `agent-client-protocol-extras` or `swissarmyhammer-agent`) that constructs a `CreateElicitationRequest` and matches on `ElicitationAction`, proving the types are reachable.
+- [ ] Run: `cargo nextest run -p agent-client-protocol-extras -p claude-agent -p llama-agent -p swissarmyhammer-agent` — all green.

 ## Workflow

 - [x] Run: `cargo nextest run -p agent-client-protocol-extras -p claude-agent -p llama-agent -p swissarmyhammer-agent` — 2428 tests, all green.

 ## Workflow
-- Use `/tdd` where it fits (the compile test first). Treat 0.11→0.12 API drift as part of this task.
\ No newline at end of file
+- Use `/tdd` where it fits (the compile test first). Treat 0.11→0.12 API drift as part of this task.
+
+## Review Findings (2026-05-22 11:56)
+
+Verification: independently confirmed every claim. Main crate `agent-client-protocol` resolves to 0.11.1 and itself pins `agent-client-protocol-schema = "=0.12.0"`; the schema crate at 0.12.0 defines `unstable_elicitation = []` and `#[cfg(feature = "unstable_elicitation")]`-gates the types (`src/lib.rs:56,74`, `src/elicitation.rs`); the main crate re-exports them via the glob `pub use agent_client_protocol_schema::*;` (`src/schema/mod.rs:229`); the main crate's `unstable` feature list does NOT forward elicitation. The test's API usage matches the schema exactly (`CreateElicitationResponse::new(action)`, `pub action: ElicitationAction`, `ElicitationAction::{Accept(_),Decline,Cancel}`, both types `#[non_exhaustive]`). All five ACP-using crates depend on `agent-client-protocol-extras`. Re-ran `cargo build -p claude-agent -p llama-agent -p agent-client-protocol-extras` (green), `cargo build --workspace` (green), and `cargo nextest run -p agent-client-protocol-extras --test elicitation_types_reachable` (1 passed). All three acceptance criteria hold. The decision to NOT bump to 0.12.1 (which pins schema `=0.13.2`) is correct and well-justified.
+
+### Warnings
+- [ ] `crates/agent-client-protocol-extras/Cargo.toml:17` (and `Cargo.toml:196`) — The `unstable_elicitation` feature is enabled only on `agent-client-protocol-extras`, and the types are reachable from `claude-agent`/`llama-agent` solely because those crates transitively depend on extras. This is correct today (resolver="2" feature unification over a single `=0.12.0` schema instance), but the feature lives in a crate logically unrelated to "I need elicitation types" — spooky action at a distance. Risk: if a future ACP-consuming crate uses elicitation but does not depend on extras (or `claude-agent`/`llama-agent` later drop their extras dep), `agent_client_protocol::schema::CreateElicitationRequest` would silently fail to resolve for that crate's own build config, while `cargo build --workspace` (which always pulls extras) stays green — so the existing reachability test would NOT catch it. Hardening (low cost, ~2 lines, not a correctness blocker): also declare `agent-client-protocol-schema = { workspace = true }` (it already carries the `unstable_elicitation` feature) directly in the `[dependencies]` of `claude-agent` and `llama-agent` so each crate is self-sufficient and enables the feature in its own build config, independent of extras. Leaving it as-is is acceptable given the current invariant holds; flagging so the choice is deliberate.
+
+### Nits
+- [ ] Task title "(schema unstable_elicitation + acp 0.12 bump)" — The acp 0.12 bump was deliberately and correctly NOT done (it would force schema `=0.13.2`). The Implementation Note documents this thoroughly, but the title still implies a bump occurred. Consider retitling to drop "+ acp 0.12 bump" so the board's summary matches what shipped.
\ No newline at end of file

The workspace dependency already carries `features = ["unstable_elicitation"]`, so `workspace = true` is sufficient. Verified `cargo build -p claude-agent -p llama-agent` and `cargo build --workspace` both green.

changes.reverse_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -39,9 +39,7 @@
 Verification: independently confirmed every claim. Main crate `agent-client-protocol` resolves to 0.11.1 and itself pins `agent-client-protocol-schema = "=0.12.0"`; the schema crate at 0.12.0 defines `unstable_elicitation = []` and `#[cfg(feature = "unstable_elicitation")]`-gates the types (`src/lib.rs:56,74`, `src/elicitation.rs`); the main crate re-exports them via the glob `pub use agent_client_protocol_schema::*;` (`src/schema/mod.rs:229`); the main crate's `unstable` feature list does NOT forward elicitation. The test's API usage matches the schema exactly (`CreateElicitationResponse::new(action)`, `pub action: ElicitationAction`, `ElicitationAction::{Accept(_),Decline,Cancel}`, both types `#[non_exhaustive]`). All five ACP-using crates depend on `agent-client-protocol-extras`. Re-ran `cargo build -p claude-agent -p llama-agent -p agent-client-protocol-extras` (green), `cargo build --workspace` (green), and `cargo nextest run -p agent-client-protocol-extras --test elicitation_types_reachable` (1 passed). All three acceptance criteria hold. The decision to NOT bump to 0.12.1 (which pins schema `=0.13.2`) is correct and well-justified.

 ### Warnings
-- [x] `crates/agent-client-protocol-extras/Cargo.toml:17` (and `Cargo.toml:196`) — The `unstable_elicitation` feature is enabled only on `agent-client-protocol-extras`, and the types are reachable from `claude-agent`/`llama-agent` solely because those crates transitively depend on extras. This is correct today (resolver="2" feature unification over a single `=0.12.0` schema instance), but the feature lives in a crate logically unrelated to "I need elicitation types" — spooky action at a distance. Risk: if a future ACP-consuming crate uses elicitation but does not depend on extras (or `claude-agent`/`llama-agent` later drop their extras dep), `agent_client_protocol::schema::CreateElicitationRequest` would silently fail to resolve for that crate's own build config, while `cargo build --workspace` (which always pulls extras) stays green — so the existing reachability test would NOT catch it. Hardening (low cost, ~2 lines, not a correctness blocker): also declare `agent-client-protocol-schema = { workspace = true }` (it already carries the `unstable_elicitation` feature) directly in the `[dependencies]` of `claude-agent` and `llama-agent` so each crate is self-sufficient and enables the feature in its own build config, independent of extras. Leaving it as-is is acceptable given the current invariant holds; flagging so the choice is deliberate.

The workspace dependency already carries `features = ["unstable_elicitation"]`, so `workspace = true` is sufficient. Verified `cargo build -p claude-agent -p llama-agent` and `cargo build --workspace` both green.

Hardening (low cost, ~2 lines, not a correctness blocker): also declare `agent-client-protocol-schema = { workspace = true }` (it already carries the `unstable_elicitation` feature) directly in the `[dependencies]` of `claude-agent` and `llama-agent` so each crate is self-sufficient and enables the feature in its own build config, independent of extras.
